text:
Admission Date:  1978-07-15              Discharge Date:   1971-09-07


Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Penicillins / Ibuprofen / Aspirin / Estrogens / Fosamax

Attending:Dominic
Chief Complaint:
Upper GI bleeding

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Endoscopy 1978-07-15


History of Present Illness:
Original H & P is as per the ICU team
.
This is a 87 year-old female with a history of afib on coumadin
who initially presented to Bayshore Medical Center-56267 William Flat
East Katherine, IL 68452-2920 with 4 days of abd pain,
one episode of melena yesterday, and coffee ground emesis this
afternoon. Hct 18, INR >10, elevated LFTs esp alk phos, and
slightly elevated amylase and lipase. The ED there was unable to
pass an NG tube for lavage but an Abd CT for associated abd pain
and distension showed a mass vs fluid collection, suspicious for
blood, arising from the duodenum at the sphincter of Oddi,
compressing the CBD with intrahepatic ductal dilatation. She
received 2 units PRBCs, FFP, vitamin K 10mg IV, and nexium 40mg
IV. She was reportedly hemodynamically stable and transferred to
Crossing Rivers Health Medical Center at the request of Dr David (Surgery) for further evaluation
and treatment.

In the ED, she has been stable. Seen by surgery, who recommended
stabilization of any intra-luminal GI bleeding and correction of
anemia and coagulopathy prior to any operative management, and
also by GI, who agreed with ICU admission and plan to scope from
above tonight. Current VS 98.6, 120, 150/88, 26, 99% 2L. Has (3)
18G PIVs, Protonix gtt running.

ROS: Positive for mild jaundice, early satiety, constipation and
also pale stools for several weeks, intense pruritis at night
for 1-2 weeks, gradual 20 lb weight loss over the last year. She
had a spider bite on her left knee about a week ago. The patient
denies any fevers, chills, chest pain, shortness of breath,
orthopnea, PND, lower extremity oedema, cough, urinary
frequency, urgency, dysuria, lightheadedness, gait unsteadiness,
focal weakness, vision changes, headache.

Past Medical History:
-Atrial fibrillation, on coumadin
-Bronchiectasis (O2 sat baseline low 90s per pt)
-Hypertension
-herpes zoster in 2005-10-11 (Right arm)

Surgical Hx:
-s/p appy
-s/p T&A


Social History:
Quit smoking tobacco ~45yrs ago, prior to that 1ppd. No ETOH.
Lives at Orange City Area Health System (7243 Blair Spurs
Littleshire, PW 90900) but independent at most
ADLs. Walks with walker/cane, wheelchair sometimes when out of
home.


Family History:
Parents died of CVAs, father with esophageal Ca, brother with
[**Name2 (NI) 499**] Ca and another brother with pancreatic Ca.


Physical Exam:
Vitals: T:97.8 BP:148/75 HR:110 RR:24 O2Sat:96% 2L
GEN: anxious thin elderly woman, friendly
James Holmes: EOMI, Matthew Stewart, + conjunctival pallor, sclera mildly
icteric, no epistaxis or rhinorrhea, MM dry, OP Clear
NECK: No JVD, carotid pulses brisk, no bruits, no cervical
lymphadenopathy, trachea midline
COR: no M/G/R, normal S1 S2, radial pulses +2, DP pulses 1+
PULM: + kyphosis. Lungs CTAB, no W/R/R
ABD: Soft, NT, ND, +BS, no HSM, diffuse firm nontender mass in
the LUQ/periumbical region
EXT: No C/C/E, no palpable cords
NEURO: alert, oriented to person, place, and time. CN II ?????? XII
grossly intact. Moves all 4 extremities. Strength 5/5 in upper
and lower extremities. Patellar DTR +1. Plantar reflex
downgoing. No cerebellar dysfunction.
SKIN: Mild jaundice esp of ext and face, no cyanosis, or gross
dermatitis. Ecchymoses at venipuncture sites on the forearms.

Pertinent Results:
1978-07-15 06:08PM   GLUCOSE-175* UREA N-44* CREAT-1.0 SODIUM-139
POTASSIUM-4.4 CHLORIDE-99 TOTAL CO2-28 ANION GAP-16
1978-07-15 06:08PM   ALT(SGPT)-175* AST(SGOT)-101* ALK PHOS-974*
TOT BILI-2.2*
1978-07-15 06:08PM   LIPASE-34
1978-07-15 06:08PM   WBC-12.2* RBC-3.52* HGB-10.1* HCT-29.4*
MCV-83 MCH-28.8 MCHC-34.6 RDW-17.2*
1978-07-15 06:08PM   NEUTS-93.0* LYMPHS-5.0* MONOS-1.8* EOS-0.1
BASOS-0.1
1978-07-15 06:08PM   PLT COUNT-218
1978-07-15 06:08PM   PT-17.1* PTT-26.8 INR(PT)-1.5*
1978-07-15 06:00PM URINE  BLOOD-TR NITRITE-NEG PROTEIN-NEG
GLUCOSE-NEG KETONE-NEG BILIRUBIN-NEG UROBILNGN-NEG PH-6.5
LEUK-NEG
1978-07-15 06:00PM URINE  RBC-0-2 WBC-0-2 BACTERIA-OCC YEAST-NONE
EPI-0-2

Laboratories from 56267 William Flat
East Katherine, IL 68452-2920:
1978-07-15 13:53 WBC 11.2, Hgb 6.1/Hct 18.1 MCV 89.4  Plts 259
90Polys, 5Lymphs, 4Monos, 1Eo
INR >10
Na 136   Cl 99  BUN 49   Glc 198
K 4.0   CO2 31   Cr 1.2

Alb 2.2, Tp 5.4, TBili 0.9, ALT 215, AST 135, AlkPhos 1419

(electrolytes and LFTS were all normal with Cr 1.0 in 07/2008)

Amylase 108, Lipase 220
CPK 38, TropT 0.018 (reference range at 56267 William Flat
East Katherine, IL 68452-2920, 0.00-0.01)

Urine Yellow, clear, small bili, ketone neg, SpecGrav 1.025, pH
6.0, trace blood, trace protein, nitrite neg; 5-10 WBCS but 12-05
epis, few bacteria and no casts or crystals.

ECG: Atrial ectopic rhythm at 125, nml axis, nml intervals, TWI
in III, aVF.

Imaging:
CXR (prelim read): Marked prominence of interstitial markings at
lung bases of unkown chronicity. Difficult to exclude airspace
consolidation at left lung base. Radiodense round foci
projecting over right upper lung may be external, though this is
unclear.

CT Abd/Pelvis (report from 56267 William Flat
East Katherine, IL 68452-2920): fluid collection vs mass
around duodenum suspicious for blood, ? arising from sphincter
of Oddi, with CBD dilatation as well as marked intra- and
extra-hepatic duct dilatation.

EGD:
Impression: Blood in the duodenum. Diverticulum in the second
part of the duodenum. Mass in the proximal second part of the
duodenum (injection). Otherwise normal EGD to second part of the
duodenum

Brief Hospital Course:
MICU COURSE:
87F with afib on coumadin presents with upper GI bleeding and
mass at the duodenum with dilated CBD.  EGD showing compressive
mass around duodenum highly suspicious for malignancy.

# Acute blood loss anema/Upper GI Source: Melena followed by
hematemesis is c/w upper GI bleeding; unable to pass NGT for
lavage at 56267 William Flat
East Katherine, IL 68452-2920, but CT scan demonstrated a large mass vs
fluid collection, density suspicious for blood, involving the
duodenum with compression of the CBD with intrahepatic ductal
dilatation; an underlying malignancy may have eroded into a
vessel, bleeding both intraluminally and out of the lumen. INR
supratherapeutic >10 at OSH which likely precipitated the large
bleed. She had been given IV Vit K and FFP at the OSH and her
INR was < 2 by arrival here.  Treated with IV PPI, now changed
to po bid.  EGD done showing mass compressing 2nd part of
duodenum highly suspicious for malignancy. It was not biopsied
during the EGD due to concern for her bleeding risk.  There was
a question of this possibly being a pancreatic mass that was
impinging on the duodenum.  Upon discussion with her about
pursuing a diagnosis she expressed a wish for palliation and did
not wish to aggressively pursue diagnosis.  She said that she
would not want to pursue surgery, chemo or radiation.  The ERCP
team was contactJeremiah Carlson and felt she could benefit from bile duct
stenting if she desired.  The patient remained asymptomatic
without abdominal pain.  She had received a total of 4 units of
PRBC (last ordered on 03-26) and her Hct remained stable the rest
of her hospitalization.

# Bile duct dilitation: Likely cause of her elevated alk phos.
EGD as above.  CA19-9 was elevated to 4936.  CEA was negative.
Patient advised of possibilities including palliative
biliary/duodenal stenting vs. biopsy for definitive diagnosis.
Patient decided not to pursue aggresive testing.

# Atrial fibrillation: Held anticoagulation 01-23 active bleeding
and held diltiazem initially given bleeding which was restarted
day after admission.  She was restarted on diltiazem as her Hct
and SBPs have been stable.  the risks and benefits of
anticoagulation were discusssed with the patient.  The patient
decided that she would want coumadin at this time to prevent
CVA.  She understood that she may start to bleed again and that
this will need to monitored closely.  She will be discharged on
a lower dose of coumadin as she came in supratherapeutic.  Also,
she is on cipro for 3 more days which can affect the INR.

# HTN: Held antihypertensives while bleeding. Her
Triamterene/HCTZ was restartd at discharge.

# UTI: The patient??????s UCx returned with 10,000- 100,000 gram neg
rods, She complete a 7 day course of cipro.

# Bronchiectasis:  the patient reports that her O2 sats at home
run between 88-92 % due to her bronchiectasis.  She was given O2
to keep her sats > 92.  She had some volume overload (acute
pulmonary edema) likely secondary to blood transfusion.  She
diuresed well with a one time dose of lasix 20mg.  Her O2 sats
should be weaned at the NH.

# Code: DNR/DNI

# Disposition:  The palliative care team was consulted.  they
spoke to the patient about goal of end of life care.  The
patient will be discharge to 448 Dana Crescent Apt. 782
South Kevinport, OH 56996-9988 for rehab before
returning to her previous nursing home.  Hospice arrangements
will be arranged for her there.

Medications on Admission:
Cardizem 120 mg twice a day
Coumadin 5 mg daily,
triamterene/hydrochlorothiazide 37.5/25 mg daily.
just completed a course of Keflex for spider bite that may have
been superinfected

Discharge Medications:
1. Trazodone 50 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO HS (at bedtime) as
needed for insomnia.
2. Cardizem CD 120 mg Capsule, Sust. Release 24 hr Sig: One (1)
Capsule, Sust. Release 24 hr PO twice a day.
3. Triamterene-Hydrochlorothiazid 37.5-25 mg Capsule Sig: One
(1) Cap PO DAILY (Daily).
4. Warfarin 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Once Daily at 4
PM.
5. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed.
6. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
7. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO Q6H (every
6 hours) as needed for fever or pain.
8. Milk Of Magnesia Concentrated 2,400 mg/10 mL Suspension Sig:
One (1)  PO every six (6) hours as needed for constipation.
9. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q12H
(every 12 hours) for 3 days.


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Avory Manor in 56267 William Flat
East Katherine, IL 68452-2920

Discharge Diagnosis:
Upper gastrointersitnal bleed
Duodenal mass
Biliary obstruction
Urinary tract Infection


Discharge Condition:
Fair


Discharge Instructions:
-Transfer to STR for further care
-Complete 3 more days of antibiotics for UTI
-Watch for signs of bleeding as coumadin has been resumed.
-Monitor INR and Hct.
-Monitor respiratory status.  Patient has bronchiectasis and her
usual O2 sat is 88-92%.  Give po lasix as needed if patient
seems volume overloaded.
-Take all other meds as prescribed
-Return to ED if you experience worsening abd pain, have blood
in your stool, nausea/vomiting, fever/chills or any other
worrisome signs/symptoms.

Followup Instructions:
Follow up with the physicians as your NH


                             Cassandra [**Initial (NamePattern1) **] Yesenia Rose MD 35334

Completed by:1971-09-07
